sections.name: Velstrac Conductor
Instruments

link.alt: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun pg. 88
link.name: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun pg. 88

sources.name: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

sections.text: Conductors go to great lengths to acquire the finest instruments for their choirs, judging an individual singer's worth by their tone, emotion, endurance, and responsiveness. Vocalists who don't measure up to a conductor's standards are either played to death or passed on to other “less discerning” velstracs. Cherished instruments can expect to live a long, tortuous existence in a conductor's choir, receiving the best medical attention after each performance—at least until their voice gives out.

sections.name: Tortured Friendships

link.alt: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun pg. 88
link.name: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun pg. 88

sources.name: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

sections.text: Velstrac conductors are fairly amiable among their kind and often work with other velstracs. They particularly get along well with ostiarius velstracs and velstrac evangelists, who they tend to boss around. Though they find sacristans piteous, they're intrigued by their Shadow Scream ability.

link.alt: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun pg. 88
link.name: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun pg. 88

sources.name: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

sections.text: Conductors are velstracs who believe screams of anguish to be the ultimate form of musical expression. These sadistic musicians compose “joyous symphonies” from the agonized howling of their tortured, captive choirs.

link.alt: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun pg. 88
link.name: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun pg. 88

sources.name: Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun

family.name: Velstrac

sections.name: Check With Players
sections.text: Velstracs are creatures of pain and mutilation. Encounters with velstracs often delve into the realm of body horror, which could be overly disturbing to some players, leading to unintentional effects on your game and your friends. Before introducing velstracs into your game, talk with your players to learn about any particular phobias or limits to determine what content you bring to the table, and consider that on your own behalf as well. Some specific elements to be aware of are velstrac themes around cutting flesh and drawing blood, especially the eremite’s abilities to remove the flesh of others and graft it to themself.

sections.name: Despicable Origins
sections.text: Velstracs originated with the first debased thoughts of mortals, which divine beings found so deplorable that they locked all velstracs away in Hell. It wasn’t long, however, before the newly formed beings escaped their infernal prison to the Netherworld.

sections.name: Other Velstracs
sections.text: There are as many different velstracs as there are ways to inflict pain. Other velstracs range in power from the relatively weak lampadariuses to the powerful phylacators.

sections.name: The Shadow Forge
sections.text: Many velstracs outsource the forging of their chains to unique velstracs known as almoners. These six-armed, legless humanoids toil endlessly at a great burning foundry called the Shadow Forge. Almoners operate under mysterious strictures and are just as likely to turn a velstrac away emptyhanded as not.

sections.name: Velstrac Divinities
sections.text: The most powerful velstracs are unique divinities known collectively as velstrac demagogues—powerful creatures worshipped by those mortals who seek to experience agonizing new revelations and gain power by enhancing the body through pain.

sections.name: Velstracs on Golarion
sections.text: Velstracs are most often encountered in the shadowy realm of Nidal, where the state worship of Zon-Kuthon is rigidly enforced. Indeed, many velstracs are created from the souls of Joyful Things, devotees of Zon-Kuthon who have had their limbs amputated to focus their minds on the veneration of pain, sacrifice, and torment.

family.text:
The shadow-dwelling fiends known as velstracs all share a horrifying preoccupation with the search for ultimate sensation through self-mutilation. Velstracs transcend their stoic detachment only when inflicting pain and terror upon their victims, practicing new forms of torture, or turning their agonizing practices back on themselves. They consider themselves enlightened beings, transcending such limitations as morality or mortal taboos, but their victims know them as emotionless tormentors who inflict sadistic suffering. These fiends claim to seek perfection in thought, form, and action, although they don’t recognize any refinement that doesn’t require the painful excision of the flesh or spirit.  
  
Velstracs manifest from the souls of the most extreme masochistic or sadistic mortals who are judged and sent on to the Netherworld. They take on forms that suit their vile predilections, ranging from the low-ranking augurs to the maestros of suffering and mutilation, the eremites. The process of transformation warps the soul step by step, with other velstracs conveying their new members through untold chambers of pain among the dark reaches of the Netherworld.

traits.name: Medium
traits.text: *Space:* 5 feet, *Reach (Tall):* 5 feet, *Reach (Long):* 5 feet

traits.name: Fiend
traits.text: Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the unholy planes are called fiends. Fiends can survive the basic environmental effects of planes in the Outer Sphere.

traits.name: Velstrac
traits.text: A family of fiends from the Netherworld that are associated with pain and agony. All velstracs possess some form of disturbing gaze.

automatic_abilities.name: Horrifying Accompaniment
automatic_abilities.text: When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it hears every scream the velstrac conductor's victims have ever uttered. The creature must succeed at a DC 25 Will save or become frightened 2 (frightened 4 on a critical failure).

traits.name: Auditory
traits.text: Auditory actions and effects rely on sound. An action with the auditory trait can be successfully performed only if the creature using the action can speak or otherwise produce the required sounds. A spell or effect with the auditory trait has its effect only if the target can hear it. This applies only to sound-based parts of the effect, as determined by the GM. This is different from a sonic effect, which still affects targets who can’t hear it (such as deaf targets) as long as the effect itself makes sound.

traits.name: Aura
traits.text: An aura is an emanation that continually ebbs out from you, affecting creatures within a certain radius. Aura can also refer to the magical signature of an item.

traits.name: Divine
traits.text:
This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to divine magic.

traits.name: Emotion
traits.text: This effect alters a creature’s emotions. Effects with this trait always have the mental trait as well. Creatures with special training or that have mechanical or artificial intelligence are immune to emotion effects.

traits.name: Fear
traits.text: Fear effects evoke the emotion of fear. Effects with this trait always have the mental and emotion traits as well.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

automatic_abilities.effect: The conductor sings a note that resonates with the triggering effect, redirecting it to the triggering creature. The triggering creature becomes the target of the triggering effect, instead of the conductor.
automatic_abilities.name: Sonic Redirection

traits.name: Divine
traits.text:
This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to divine magic.

traits.name: Sonic
traits.text: An effect with the sonic trait functions only if it makes sound, meaning it has no effect in an area of silence or in a vacuum. This is different from an auditory spell, which is effective only if the target can hear it. A sonic effect might deal sonic damage.

automatic_abilities.trigger: A creature targets the velstrac conductor with an auditory or sonic effect or includes the conductor in the area of an auditory or sonic effect

modifiers.name: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

interaction_abilities.name: Painsight
interaction_abilities.text: A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the doomed, dying, and wounded conditions, as well as the value of those conditions.

traits.name: Divine
traits.text:
This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to divine magic.

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Finesse
traits.text: You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls using this melee weapon. You still calculate damage using Strength.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

traits.name: Divine
traits.text:
This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to divine magic.

traits.name: Range
traits.text: These attacks will either list a finite range or a range increment, which follows the normal rules for range increments.

traits.name: Sonic
traits.text: An effect with the sonic trait functions only if it makes sound, meaning it has no effect in an area of silence or in a vacuum. This is different from an auditory spell, which is effective only if the target can hear it. A sonic effect might deal sonic damage.

ability.name: Focus Gaze
ability.text: The velstrac conductor stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must immediately attempt a Will save against the velstrac conductor's horrifying accompaniment aura. In addition, if the creature was already frightened, on a failed save the creature takes 1d6 persistent mental damage. After attempting this save, the creature is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

traits.name: Divine
traits.text:
This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to divine magic.

traits.name: Emotion
traits.text: This effect alters a creature’s emotions. Effects with this trait always have the mental trait as well. Creatures with special training or that have mechanical or artificial intelligence are immune to emotion effects.

traits.name: Fear
traits.text: Fear effects evoke the emotion of fear. Effects with this trait always have the mental and emotion traits as well.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

ability.effect:
With a horrifying flourish and hungry smile, the velstrac conductor points their baton at a creature within 100 feet, compelling them to scream. The creature must attempt a DC 28 Will save. On a failure, they scream, taking 4d6 mental damage (8d6 on a critical failure) and dealing 2d6 sonic damage to all creatures in a 20-foot emanation (DC 28 basic Fortitude save).   
Instead of targeting one creature, the conductor can choose to target all creatures in range that were compelled to scream by this ability last round.
ability.name: Scream for Me

traits.name: Divine
traits.text:
This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to divine magic.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

traits.name: Sonic
traits.text: An effect with the sonic trait functions only if it makes sound, meaning it has no effect in an area of silence or in a vacuum. This is different from an auditory spell, which is effective only if the target can hear it. A sonic effect might deal sonic damage.

ability.name: Unholy Exaltation
ability.text: The conductor lets out a terrible scream of joy, dealing 10d6 sonic damage to all creatures in either a 30-foot cone or a 90-foot line (DC 28 basic Will save). Holy creatures that fail this save are also sickened 2. The conductor can't use Unholy Exaltation again for 1d4 rounds.
